Irving, Texas, police charged Magdaleno Roman-Rosales, of Mexico, with kidnapping many years ago and he ultimately received a 120-day sentence for unlawful restraint. He was subsequently removed to his home country. At some point, he returned to the United States illegally. 

🔎ICE @ERODallas Fugitive Operations arrested him during a targeted immigration enforcement operation near his residence May 8. During the arrest, he put our officers, the public and himself in danger by attempting to flee.
 
🚨He backed his truck into an ICE vehicle. Before he was able to drive away, skilled ICE officers were able to gain entry into the vehicle. Then, Roman-Rosales refused to exit his truck and had to be physically removed from the cab. He continued to resist his arrest at every step. Thankfully, no one was seriously injured during the operation. 

➡️He will stay in ICE custody pending prosecution.

rows.text:
As a former federal law enforcement officer, I share Virginians' concerns about federal immigration enforcement officers wearing masks on American streets — undercutting basic expectations of accountability, sowing fear and confusion, and eroding public trust.

Kids afraid to get on the school bus. Workers not showing up to their jobs. Neighbors scared to go to the grocery store.

That's what aggressive federal immigration enforcement is doing to Virginia communities.

Today, I signed Executive Order 16 to:
🔹 Ban ICE from using state property as an operations base
🔹 Require federal immigration officers to show warrants before accessing state property
🔹 Launch a Know Your Rights website for Virginians affected by federal immigration enforcement to access resources and guidance
🔹 Provide guidance for employees at courthouses, schools, polling locations, and healthcare facilities when federal officers attempt to conduct immigration enforcement

He continued to resist his arrest at every step. Thankfully, no one was seriously injured during the operation. ➡️He will stay in ICE custody pending prosecution.
